title: Living in Boulder Vlog 2 - 4 - 2016 + Drug Test Results 71.6 Nanograms = Sober for a Stoner

enrichment.summary: Kyle shares the results of a self-funded blood THC test he took after smoking 70 hits of Kim Dog cannabis over roughly five hours: he registered 71.7 nanograms of marijuana parent metabolite while feeling essentially sober. He uses the result to argue that Colorado's 5-nanogram legal driving limit is absurdly low and that employer drug testing unfairly bars habitual medical marijuana users — including himself — from jobs like night-shift supermarket stocking.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Far too low — Kyle tested at 71.7 ng while functionally sober, so the limit criminalizes sober habitual users
Unfair and should be abolished — bars competent workers from jobs they could perform fine
Unsure it's worth renewing given rising prices and diminishing discount benefits

stances.topic: Night-shift supermarket stocking as backup job
stances.position: Would be ideal for him — low social interaction, peaceful, suits his lifestyle — but drug testing blocks it
Mid-range (15–20% THC), not particularly strong compared to strains like White Widow at 27%

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got my drug test results back after about a week.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I took the drug test on January 25th and got the results around February 5th.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This video will discuss drug tests and marijuana-related topics.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have medical marijuana that expires on September 16th of this year.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm unsure if I'll renew my medical marijuana card because it's expensive.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My recent half-ounce purchase of medical marijuana cost $95, which is more than before.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The non-medical marijuana I bought previously was better quality.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I tested positive for marijuana in my blood test with a level of 2206.5 nanograms for metabolites and 71.7 nanograms for parent marijuana.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The doctor confirmed the results were confidential but would be reported.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I smoked 70 hits of marijuana over five hours before the blood test.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I felt pretty much sober despite the high marijuana levels in my blood.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The legal limit for marijuana in blood tests is 5 nanograms, making my result 71.7 problematic.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A friend got a DUI for weed with a level of 50 nanograms, even though he wasn't high that day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe medical marijuana licenses should be expanded to avoid legal issues for users.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I accidentally revealed my address in the video and will need to blur it out.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think marijuana users should have a special designation on driver's licenses to avoid legal problems.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm considering a nighttime supermarket stocker job because it suits my lifestyle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The supermarket I'm interested in used to be open 24 hours but reduced hours due to theft and worker shortages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I feel frustrated that jobs require drug testing, which excludes marijuana users.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe stoners could excel in jobs like nighttime stocking or gardening.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think society unfairly accuses people of not wanting to work while also restricting their job opportunities.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to start a petition to advocate for more job opportunities for marijuana users.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I mentioned a homeless friend who struggled to find work due to alcoholism and poor job application skills.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to make it easier for people to find jobs that suit their lifestyles.
